wf_progress_unparsed: Could not interpret progress (the CLI display format may have changed)
hub_stopped: Web stopped — restart with many-ai-cli serve
project_favorite_add: Pin project to top (add to favorites)
settings_display_name_desc: Falls back to system username if empty. Shown as the initial letter in the avatar circle.
settings_avatar_url_desc: Local path or URL. Falls back to initial circle if not set or image fails to load.
settings_session_log_desc: OFF by default — recommended. When ON, every session is recorded to disk (.log / .jsonl / .txt and the SQLite history), and any confidential information you type or paste during the session — passwords, API keys, tokens — is written to the logs as well. These cannot be reliably masked. Turn this ON only if you understand and accept that risk. That is why OFF is recommended. Applies to new sessions. Note: while OFF, the Chat history tab and split view still show the current session's conversation live (rendered directly from the terminal stream). Disk persistence — past-session browsing and scrollback restore on reconnect — is disabled.
settings_attach_cleanup_desc: Old attachments are deleted by retention days; total size is capped (0 disables). Cleaned at startup and every 6 hours.
settings_history_reset_desc: Deletes SQLite search/chat history. Running sessions continue.
settings_history_reset_confirm_message: Delete the session history stored in SQLite? Running sessions will continue, but saved search/chat history cannot be restored.
settings_logs_purge_desc: Deletes all session logs (.log/.jsonl/.txt), spawn logs, and SQLite history. Running sessions are kept.
settings_logs_purge_confirm_message: Delete all saved logs? Logs of running sessions are kept, but everything else cannot be restored.
legacy_logs_notice_title: Reset recommended for the new version
legacy_logs_notice_message: This new version turns session logging OFF by default. For a clean start, deleting data left from earlier versions is recommended (the raw .log files may contain UNMASKED API keys, tokens, or passwords). Choose what to reset, then Execute. Data of running sessions is kept.
legacy_logs_notice_opt_enable: Record session logs from now on — confidential info you type (passwords, API keys, tokens) will be written to the logs. Enable only if you accept this risk; OFF is recommended.
settings_attachments_purge_desc: Deletes all saved attachment folders. Folders of running sessions are kept.
settings_attachments_purge_confirm_messa: Delete all saved attachments? Attachments of running sessions are kept, but everything else cannot be restored.
settings_token_statusbar_desc: Shows cost, model, and elapsed time for the active session at the bottom of the screen.
tsb_turn_elapsed_title: Time the AI has been working since the latest send or approval response
tsb_ailines_title: Lines edited by AI this session (distinct from working-tree git diff)
tsb_sent_history_hint: Click to view sent history in chronological order
tsb_sent_history_attach_hint: Click to open (preview / default app / folder, etc.)
settings_approval_auto_switch_desc: Automatically switch to sessions waiting for approval. If the current session is waiting for approval, switching waits until it is resolved.
settings_approval_hub_desc: Operate AI approvals via on-screen buttons. When enabled, injects rules into each AI's instruction file.
settings_desktop_notifications_desc: Show OS notifications for pending approvals. Suppressed for the current visible session.
settings_push_notifications_desc: Notify pending approvals even after the tab is closed. Notification payloads are sent through the browser push service; the Hub token is not included.
settings_ntfy_desc: Receive approval notifications on your phone even when the SSH tunnel is disconnected. The Hub token is never included in the payload.
settings_done_summary_notify_desc: Send a summary notification when AI completes a task. Requires approval rules to be enabled.
push_notification_pwa_required: Enable from the Home Screen PWA
settings_quick_cmd_placeholder: e.g. create a bugfix / slash command
settings_quick_cmd_show_tooltip: Show this command as a button
settings_input_tools_desc: Toggle the /clear, /model, / ▾ and ⌫ buttons in the input bar per screen width. Default: shown on PC, hidden on mobile (to save space).
settings_approval_patterns_desc: Phrases used to detect CLI approval prompts. Edit if a CLI message changes or false positives occur.
toast_input_deferred: Session #{id}: input held (wrapper not connected). It will be sent automatically on reconnect.
toast_model_blocked_on_ollama: /model is disabled for sessions launched on Ollama or LM Studio. Start a new session to switch models.
toast_select_menu_active: A select menu is open. Pick an option from the buttons below or in the terminal pane (your input was held).
shell_ai_launch_msg: You are about to launch {provider} inside a shell session. The shell-tuned input/approval handling will mismatch the AI CLI, causing garbled slash commands and broken approval buttons. We recommend opening a dedicated {provider} session instead.
user_prefs_save_failed: Settings save failed (server unreachable). Changes saved locally.
user_prefs_save_failed_unauthorized: Settings save failed (stale auth token). Reopen this tab. Changes saved locally.
user_prefs_save_failed_server: Settings save failed (Hub error HTTP {status}). Changes saved locally.
user_prefs_save_failed_http: Settings save failed (HTTP {status}). Changes saved locally.
user_prefs_save_failed_network: Settings save failed (cannot reach Hub). Changes saved locally.
settings_usage_links_desc: Header usage link destinations. Leave blank to use the default URL.
spawn_cwd_missing_btn: Cannot launch: working directory does not exist
spawn_model_provider_mismatch: This model is not available for the selected provider.
spawn_claude_note: Uses the installed Claude Code. Sign-in, quota, and permission policy stay in Claude.
spawn_codex_note: Uses the installed Codex CLI. Sign-in, quota, and permission policy stay in Codex.
spawn_copilot_note: Uses the installed GitHub Copilot CLI. Sign-in, quota, and permission policy stay in Copilot.
spawn_cursor_agent_note: Uses the installed Cursor Agent CLI. Sign-in, quota, and permission policy stay in Cursor Agent.
spawn_opencode_note: Uses the installed OpenCode CLI. Run `opencode auth login` before starting. Free-tier models may take 1–2 minutes to respond.
spawn_grok_note: Uses the installed Grok Build CLI (xAI). Requires a SuperGrok or X Premium+ subscription — the base X Premium plan cannot use it. Sign-in and quota stay in Grok.
model_summary_cost: Cost: Model changes may affect pricing.
model_summary_compat: Compatibility: Model changes may alter behavior/compatibility.
model_require_confirm: I understand and want to proceed with high-risk settings
codex_model_summary_permission: Permission: danger-full-access / never are high risk.
codex_model_confirm_message: This session includes unrestricted access or no approval prompts. Launch it anyway?
claude_model_summary_permission: Permission: bypassPermissions skips all approvals (high risk).
claude_model_confirm_message: This session includes bypassPermissions or a model change. Launch it anyway?
spawn_preview_not_available: This preset requires Shell support (C4). Use Single large pane for now.
approval_dismiss_tooltip: Hide the approval popup (restore with re-show; approval stays pending)
about_description: A tool to centrally manage approval operations and progress monitoring for multiple AI coding CLIs in one web UI. All processing is done locally.
about_license_text: MANY-AI-CLI is distributed under the MIT License. Copyright (c) 2026 Hiroshi Ishizaka (ishizakahiroshi).
about_oss_xterm_desc: MIT License. Used as the terminal emulator.
about_oss_fit_desc: MIT License. Used for automatic terminal size adjustment.
about_oss_unicode_desc: MIT License. Used for Unicode 11 character width support.
about_oss_links_desc: MIT License. Used for URL link detection in terminal.
about_oss_webgl_desc: MIT License. Used for cell-grid-aligned terminal rendering (WebGL).
about_oss_marked_desc: MIT License. Used as the Markdown parser for the files preview.
about_oss_dompurify_desc: Apache-2.0 / MPL-2.0 License. Used to sanitize HTML rendered by the files preview.
about_oss_highlight_desc: BSD-3-Clause License. Used for syntax highlighting in the files preview.
about_license_note_pre: Full license text is in the repository
about_thirdparty_text: The mobile-connect wizard suggests third-party apps (Termius, Tailscale, WireGuard) only as examples of clients that work with this setup. These are independent products; many-ai-cli is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by their developers. You may use any equivalent app, and you install and use third-party software at your own risk. All product names are trademarks or registered trademarks of their respective owners. WireGuard is a registered trademark of Jason A. Donenfeld.
about_oss_pty_desc: MIT License. Used for PTY control on Unix.
about_oss_gopty_desc: MIT License. Used for PTY control on Windows (ConPTY).
about_oss_goterm_desc: BSD-3-Clause License. Used for terminal control.
about_oss_lumberjack_desc: MIT License. Used for log rotation.
about_oss_yaml_desc: MIT + Apache-2.0 License. Used for parsing YAML configuration.
about_oss_webpush_desc: MIT License. Used for Web Push notifications.
about_oss_sqlite_desc: BSD-3-Clause License. Used for local session history storage.
about_oss_whisper_cpp_desc: MIT License. Copyright (c) 2023-2026 The ggml authors. Local speech-to-text engine for the bundled voice input.
about_oss_ggml_desc: MIT License. Copyright (c) 2023-2026 The ggml authors. Tensor library underlying whisper.cpp.
about_oss_whisper_model_desc: MIT License. Copyright (c) 2022 OpenAI. Model weights downloaded at runtime for transcription.
about_oss_vc_runtime_desc: © Microsoft Corporation. Microsoft Visual Studio License Terms (not open source). Redistributed on Windows x64 so the bundled whisper-server runs without a separate VC++ install.
multi_question_banner: Multi-question prompt — on-screen buttons can't drive this. Use ←/→ to switch tabs, ↑/↓ to select, Space to toggle multi-select, Enter to confirm in the terminal.
stale_binary_banner: ⚠ This Hub is running an OLD build — the on-disk executable has changed since it started. Your rebuild won't take effect until the Hub is restarted (e.g. `many-ai-cli stop`, then relaunch).
approval_batch_detail_empty: Select an option to see its details here.
approval_confirm_payload_label: Text sent to the terminal (question# option# / custom is text):
action_bar_collapse: Compact view (truncate question and options to one line)
approval_toast_msg:
Enable approval buttons?

When enabled, AI agent approvals can be operated via on-screen buttons. Rules are injected into each AI's instruction file and automatically removed when the last active session for that file ends or many-ai-cli stops.

You can change this setting anytime in the settings panel.
voice_wakeword_armed: All sessions armed – hover over input to start (Alt+W to disable)
voice_wakeword_listening: Listening for wake word... (click to stop)
voice_wakeword_session_tooltip: Wake word standby – this session only
voice_wakeword_session_armed: This session armed – hover over input to start listening
voice_error_audio_capture: No audio from microphone (check device selection)
voice_error_network: Cannot connect to the speech recognition service
voice_error_service: Browser speech recognition service is unavailable
voice_error_language: Speech recognition does not support this language
voice_error_no_result: Could not recognize speech (microphone may be contested). Please try again
voice_whisper_discard_silence: No speech was detected, so transcription was skipped
settings_voice_input_privacy: Uses the browser's built-in recognition: audio is sent to the browser vendor's speech servers (Google / Microsoft). Turn this OFF if you do not want audio leaving your machine (Alt+V is disabled too).
settings_voice_engine_desc_off: Hide the microphone button and disable Alt+V.
settings_voice_engine_desc_browser: Use the browser's built-in recognition. Audio is sent to the browser vendor's speech servers (Google / Microsoft).
settings_voice_engine_desc_whisper: Record on this device and send audio through the Hub to a local Whisper server for recognition on the PC.
settings_voice_browser_unavailable: Browser recognition is unavailable on this device. Choose Whisper or OFF.
settings_voice_whisper_config_desc: Uses voice.whisper.server_url from the Hub config.yaml. If it is not configured, recording will show a configuration error.
settings_voice_whisper_status_unsupporte: Managed install is available on Windows x64 only.
settings_voice_whisper_note_download: The installer downloads whisper.cpp from GitHub Releases and the selected model from Hugging Face. Binary archives are SHA-256 verified; models without a published hash are downloaded over HTTPS and marked as hash-unverified.
settings_voice_whisper_note_ready: Installed at {path}. Start the server, then select Whisper as the engine.
settings_voice_whisper_auto_stop_desc: Automatically finalizes the recording and starts transcription after the silence set in "End-detection wait time" elapses following speech. When OFF, recording continues until you press the confirm button.
settings_voice_whisper_auto_submit_desc: Whisper can hallucinate on silence or short sounds. To avoid accidental sends, results are inserted into the input field by default.
settings_voice_whisper_external_desc: If an external API URL is configured, audio data is sent to that external service.
settings_voice_grace_desc: Wait time (seconds) before finalizing recognition after speech stops. Whisper auto-finalizes after this much silence. With Chrome's built-in engine the browser's own end detection takes precedence.
voice_diag_running: Diagnostics running. Say a short phrase and wait until recognition finishes.
voice_diag_permission_denied: Microphone permission is denied. Check the Chrome site setting for 127.0.0.1.
voice_diag_audio_capture_failed: Could not capture microphone audio. Check the OS and Chrome input device.
voice_diag_speech_service_failed: Chrome speech recognition service is unreachable or not allowed.
voice_diag_profile_or_stt_stuck_suspecte: Suspected Chrome normal-profile or speech-service stuck state. No result/end/error arrived after audioend.
voice_diag_normal_profile_specific: Treat this as a normal Chrome profile specific issue. If Incognito or a new profile works, Chrome profile state is more likely than many-ai-cli code.
voice_diag_no_result: Recognition ended without a result. If this was not no-speech, continue with environment comparison.
voice_diag_unsupported: This browser cannot run Web Speech API diagnostics. Try a Chromium-based browser.
voice_diag_copy_failed: Failed to copy voice diagnostics log
voice_diag_guide_1: Open the same Hub URL in Incognito, allow microphone access, and try voice input.
voice_diag_guide_2: If Incognito works, try the same Hub URL in a new Chrome profile.
voice_diag_guide_3: In the normal profile, reset only the microphone permission for 127.0.0.1, then allow it again.
voice_diag_guide_4: If it still fails, delete LocalStorage, SessionStorage, cookies, and cache separately, and note which reset recovers it.
voice_diag_guide_5: As the last reset, delete all site data for 127.0.0.1. Paste chrome://settings/content/all?searchSubpage=127.0.0.1 into the address bar.
voice_diag_guide_6: If it still fails, copy the diagnostics log and report it.
settings_voice_wakeword_desc: Keep microphone in standby and start voice input only when this phrase is spoken.
slash_picker_not_configured: Source URL not configured (set it in Settings)
settings_slash_src_note: Official docs are used automatically. Override the URL only when needed.
settings_idle_timeout_desc: 0 = never. AI sessions are terminated after this many minutes with no browser connected.
settings_reconnect_grace_desc: 0 = disable reconnect. How long the wrapper keeps claude/codex alive while waiting for the Hub to come back after a crash. Terminates the PTY if the Hub does not return. Applies to NEW sessions only — running sessions keep the value they were spawned with.
settings_revoke_all_desc: Regenerates the Hub token and auth cookie, kicking out every device including a lost one. This PC will also need to re-login at a new URL. SSH/VPN keys must be revoked separately (see the lost-device playbook).
settings_remote_pin_desc: Off by default. When set, non-loopback (phone/VPN) access additionally requires a PIN login. Loopback (this PC) keeps passing with the token only. Intended as an extra lock for VPN-only setups that don't expose SSH.
settings_remote_pin_format: PIN must be 6 or more digits
settings_remote_pin_clear_msg: This clears the remote PIN. Non-loopback access will then pass with the token only. Continue?
revoke_all_confirm_message: Regenerates the Hub token and auth cookie, kicking every device (including a lost one) off the Hub. This PC will also need to re-login at a new URL. SSH/VPN keys must be revoked separately. Continue?
pin_login_msg: This Hub is protected by a remote PIN. Please enter your PIN.
pin_login_locked: Too many attempts. Try again in {sec} seconds.
pin_login_locked_generic: Too many attempts. Please wait and try again.
session_history_reset_tooltip: Reset display history for this session
session_history_reset_confirm: Reset display history for this session? The running AI session will not be stopped.
settings_file_open_desc: File paths from the terminal and Files open with the OS default app. Only the terminal app can be configured.
settings_terminal_app_desc: Empty = OS default (Win: Windows Terminal → PowerShell)
link_delete_dir_confirm: Delete folder "{name}" and all of its contents?
link_open_default_error: Failed to open. Check your OS default app settings.
files_tree_empty: No files found in this directory (hidden files, node_modules, vendor, etc. are excluded)
files_preview_readonly_note: 🔒 Outside the project scope — shown read-only (mentioned in chat).
files_preview_forbidden_scope: 🔒 This file is outside the project scope. It cannot be previewed because it was not referenced in your own input (paths that appear only in AI output are excluded for safety).
git_pull_confirm_message: Pull (fast-forward) from upstream and update the working tree. Continue?
git_pull_diverged: Branch has diverged — manual merge needed
git_push_confirm_message: Push {ahead} commit(s) from {branch} to the configured upstream. This publishes commits to the remote and cannot be undone locally. Continue?
git_push_rejected: Remote has new commits. Pull first.
git_commit_no_push_warning: Only git add -A and git commit are run. Push is not run.
git_commit_open_toast: Commit all will stage all current working tree changes with git add -A, then commit them. Push is not run.
git_commit_generate_note: Generate has many-ai-cli analyze the diff and fill in a draft commit message. This is a lightweight heuristic, so accuracy is limited. Review it before committing.
git_commit_review_ready: Review complete. Commit will include all current working tree changes.
git_commit_disabled_no_subject: Enter a commit message subject first.
git_commit_ai_note: Ask the connected AI agent to read the diff and draft a commit message. The result is captured automatically from the AI's reply. Review it before committing.
git_commit_ai_timeout: Timed out waiting for the AI response.
git_commit_ai_failed: AI failed to produce a commit message.
git_commit_identity_missing: Git user.name / user.email is not configured.
git_view_select_row_hint: Click a row to see commit detail
card_ctx_title: Context window usage (low headroom slows responses and triggers auto-compact)
card_elapsed_title: Elapsed time of the current response (since this turn started)
card_longproc_title: This response has been running a long time. It may be stuck behind a heavy turn (large context × high effort). Send ESC in the terminal to interrupt instead of resending.
approval_tab_multiq_hint: Multi-question prompt — please operate from the terminal
hv_open_tooltip: Show output older than the scrollback from the session log
btn_expand_all_tooltip: Expand all tool calls (enabled in C4)
btn_collapse_all_tooltip: Collapse all tool calls (enabled in C4)
settings_locked_mode_desc: Opens new sessions and reloads in the selected mode. You can still switch manually.
toast_locked_mode_switched: Locked to "{mode}" in settings. Will revert on new session / reload.
ollama_encoding_warn_message: PowerShell input encoding is not UTF-8. Japanese input to Ollama CLI may be garbled. Switch to UTF-8 for this session?
files_preview_edit_conflict_msg: This file was modified by another process
files_tree_new_file_error_already_exists: A file or folder with that name already exists
files_tree_new_file_error_forbidden: Creating files here is not allowed
files_tree_new_folder_error_already_exis: A file or folder with that name already exists
files_tree_new_folder_error_forbidden: Creating folders here is not allowed
server_no_profiles: No connection profiles yet. Add one below.
server_connected: Connected to {name}. Opening in a new tab.
server_add_name_duplicate: A profile with this name already exists
server_fetch_hint: Enter host / user / key, then pull the remote's own info over SSH to fill the form. The key stays as you typed it.
server_fetch_done: Filled from remote. Review and Save.
server_fetch_key_missing: No key set — fill it in if this host needs one.
mobile_connect_warnbar: ⚠️ A QR with the token is equivalent to a password. Never share screenshots or your screen.
mobile_connect_consent_body:
If your connection info leaks, a third party can remotely control your PC/server, steal/alter/delete files, and run arbitrary commands. In the worst case your machine is fully compromised.
・A URL/QR with the token is the password itself. Never screenshot, screen-share, or post it.
・Exposing SSH / configuring a VPN / changing allowed_hosts is entirely at your own risk.
・Never use public Wi-Fi (cafes, airports, hotels, etc.).
・This software comes with absolutely no warranty.
mobile_connect_consent_agree: I understand the risks above (skipped next time)
mobile_connect_help_sub: There is a trade-off between ease of setup and available features.
mobile_connect_help_tip: The key question: do you want notifications pushed to your phone? View/approve only → VPN raw IP (zero effort). Want notifications → VPN+HTTPS or SSH tunnel.
mobile_connect_q1: ① How do you want to connect?
mobile_connect_opt_ssh_desc: SSH tunnel. No account or install, instant at hand
mobile_connect_opt_vpn_desc: Tailscale. Just install the app once — home or away, no port forwarding
mobile_connect_done_sub: Once your tunnel/VPN is up, scanning this opens the Hub.
mobile_connect_q2_sub: We show install steps for your phone.
mobile_connect_q3: ③ Install the app (first time only)
mobile_connect_q3_sub: Scanning this QR with {os} opens the official download page.
mobile_connect_thirdparty_note: These are independent third-party apps, not made by many-ai-cli. Review and use them at your own discretion.
mobile_connect_ssh_ctx_extra: SSH tunnel goes through 127.0.0.1, so all features work.
mobile_connect_ssh_steps: ① Import this SSH info into the app and connect → ② scan the URL QR below
mobile_connect_ssh_note: ⚠️ The QR is for importing the host. Port forwarding (-L) must be set in the app or use the command above.
mobile_connect_ssh_url_cap: Scan this after the tunnel is up (password-equivalent)
mobile_connect_ts_steps: Log into Tailscale → turn ON → scan the URL QR below
mobile_connect_ts_ctx_https: (assumes a Hub published over https via tailscale serve)
mobile_connect_ts_ctx_rawip: To open by raw IP you must also add that IP to allowed_hosts in config.
mobile_connect_wg_paste_sub: Paste your WireGuard client config → QR is generated on the spot (not stored on the server).
mobile_connect_wg_placeholder: Paste a config to show its QR.
mobile_connect_wg_qr_cap: Import the pasted config into the WireGuard app
mobile_connect_wg_after: After the VPN is connected, open the Hub:
mobile_connect_wg_ctx_https: (HTTPS requires a separate reverse proxy / certificate)
mobile_connect_wg_ctx_rawip: To open by raw IP you must also add it to allowed_hosts.
mobile_connect_vpn_url_cap_rawip: http://127.0.0.1 Hub URL (over VPN, limited features)
mobile_connect_ctx_secure: ✅ secure context (localhost or HTTPS). Push notifications, voice input, and add-to-home all work.
mobile_connect_ctx_insecure: ⚠️ Direct raw-IP VPN access is not a secure context, so notifications, voice input, and add-to-home are unavailable (viewing/approving still works). To push notifications to your phone, use HTTPS or an SSH tunnel.
mobile_connect_notify_unsupported: This device does not support notifications
mobile_connect_notify_body: Notification test: if you see this, it works
mobile_connect_home_ios: Add to home (iOS): Share button → "Add to Home Screen"
mobile_connect_home_generic: Add to home: iOS Share → "Add to Home Screen"; Android Menu → "Install app"
mobile_connect_error_unauthorized: Authentication failed (invalid token). Please reload the page.
mobile_connect_error_network: Network error. Cannot reach the Hub.
mobile_connect_error_no_lan_ip: Could not detect a LAN IP. Check your network connection (SSH/VPN steps may need manual entry).
mobile_connect_sshd_unknown: Could not determine OpenSSH server status
mobile_connect_sshd_enable_hint: On Windows add "Settings → Optional features → OpenSSH Server" and start the sshd service (on mac/Linux enable Remote Login).
mobile_connect_ts_remote_note: ℹ️ serve runs on this remote host. The QR points to the remote's tailnet name.
mobile_connect_ts_not_installed: Tailscale is missing on the PC (Hub side). Install Tailscale on the machine running the Hub and sign in with the same account.
mobile_connect_ts_degrade_ssh: ⚠️ The tailscale CLI is unreachable in this environment (e.g. Docker). Please connect via an SSH tunnel instead.
mobile_connect_ts_not_logged_in: Tailscale is installed but not logged in / not ON. Sign in and turn the connection ON.
mobile_connect_ts_serve_disabled: HTTPS (serve) is not yet enabled on your tailnet. Enable it in the admin console.
mobile_connect_ts_funnel_warn: ⚠️ On the enable screen, be sure to uncheck Funnel (Funnel exposes you to the whole internet). This enables tailnet-only access.
mobile_connect_ts_admin_open: Enable HTTPS in the admin console
mobile_connect_ts_serve_inactive: Almost there. Enable tailscale serve to get a real connection QR.
mobile_connect_ts_equiv_cmd: Advanced: you can also run the equivalent command yourself.
mobile_connect_ts_toolbar_hint: Enable external access from the “🌐 Expose” button in the toolbar.
mobile_connect_ts_toolbar_stop_hint: Stop publishing from the “🌐 Expose” button in the toolbar.
mobile_connect_ts_ready_sub: serve is enabled. Scan this QR to open the Hub securely from anywhere (tailnet-only).
mobile_connect_ts_ctx_https: (Served over HTTPS via tailscale serve — secure context, full features)
mobile_connect_ts_no_url: Could not get the real URL. Please retry.
mobile_connect_ts_serve_disabled_toast: Enable HTTPS on your tailnet, then retry.
mobile_connect_wg_ip_prompt: Enter the WG server IP to show the connection URL QR.
mobile_connect_wg_url_cap: Scan this after connecting the VPN (password-equivalent)
mobile_connect_wg_url_note: ⚠️ Plain HTTP over a raw IP is not a secure context, so notifications/voice/PWA are limited. HTTPS (reverse proxy / certificate) is recommended.
